evals.prompt: I need to add OAuth authentication to my Next.js app. It must support Google and GitHub as providers, protect admin routes, and store the session in a secure cookie.
evals.expected_output: The workflow activates REFERENCE mode with multi-agent planning: Planner decomposes into subtasks (OAuth config, providers, middleware, session), Implementer codes, Reviewer verifies security, Tester runs tests.
assertions: Uses the structured handoff protocol with TASK, COMPLEXITY, SKILLS, ACCEPTANCE
Includes COMPLEXITY: high in the handoff
The Reviewer verifies auth and cookie security
The flow follows the order: Plan -> Implement -> Test -> Review
Lessons are recorded in ### [CATEGORY] Title format

evals.prompt: The contact form doesn't send emails. Users report it stays loading forever after submit.
evals.expected_output: The workflow identifies this as a bug-fix, delegates investigation to the Planner for diagnosis, then Implementer fixes, Tester verifies the fix.
assertions: Uses the handoff protocol with relevant LESSONS_FILTER
The Implementer investigates the root cause before patching
The Tester verifies the form works post-fix
New lessons are recorded in lessons.md with ### [CATEGORY] Title format

evals.prompt: I want to optimize my workflow costs. I'm spending too many premium requests on simple tasks.
evals.expected_output: The skill provides guidance on COMPLEXITY levels and recommends strategies based on task classification.
assertions: Explains low, medium, and high complexity profiles as options
Mentions the context minimization strategy as an optimization foundation
Presents the two-layer skill system for progressive disclosure
